histories.memo:
3/18: Got Yingying's email: (email title: Incident Report: Yingying Wang (FAC25004))
I am writing to provide some feedback and seek clarification regarding the latest performance report. Please see my points below:
Regarding the Occurrence date on 3/16/2026: The report shows activity on Monday, 3/16, but I did not work at all that day. I would like to know how these records were generated. I started worked on 3/17 (Tuesday) this week, and my only action was updating previously unfinished tasks to "Completed" status.
Request for specific modification details: Could you please provide a detailed list of exactly what content the system shows I modified? This will help me verify if there is a system error or other discrepancy.
Issues with ClickUp links: Regarding the ClickUp links provided in your spreadsheet, while the links are accessible, they do not show any items or data related to the time or tasks I actually modified. Could you please double-check the source of these records?
Thank you for your assistance in clarifying these matters. I look forward to your reply.
3/18: HR replied with reason. 
3/18: Yingying replied: Regarding the task timing, I would like to clarify that this task belongs in Week 12.
Due to the recurring settings created prior to the standardization of ClickUp, a timing error occurred that caused the task to incorrectly show as overdue. Since we are currently only in Week 11, I simply adjusted the date to the correct Week 12 deadline (March 28, 2026).
If you truly believe that correcting a date to its proper range constitutes an unauthorized modification that warrants a report to the supervisor, then I have nothing more to say...
In my view, reporting such a necessary correction of a system error only serves to waste everyone’s time and is a complete misplacement of priorities.
Furthermore, if you still consider this a mistake, I honestly do not care. please keep it
3/18: HR replied with reason.
